title: drymocallis glandulosa subsp. ewanii
intro: Drymocallis glandulosa subsp. ewanii, also known as Ewan's drymocallis or Ewan's twinflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Drymocallis glandulosa subsp. ewanii include its potential to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and treat wounds, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, can be made from the leaves and roots, and are often used to treat a variety of ailments.
